extraction.one_line_summary: MERLIER Tim won stage 6 with perfectly timed sprint after HAGEN Carl Fredrik solo breakaway lasted 160km, holding off ACKERMANN Pascal and CORT Magnus
tactical_patterns: Israel Start-up Nation controlled peloton despite QUICKSTEP having numbers, suggesting they calculated leadout positioning over raw train strength.
HAGEN Carl Fredrik's 160km solo attempt was team-supported by Lotto until mathematically futile, extracting maximum sponsor exposure.
GAVIRIA Fernando launched earliest but faded, indicating leadout trains starting too far out neutralize pure speed on coastal drags.

rider_signals.MERLIER Tim: positive: recovered from stage 1 crash to win with superior positioning over faster pure sprinters
rider_signals.HAGEN Carl Fredrik: positive: 160km solo shows exceptional diesel engine for future breakaway calculus
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: holding points jersey but beaten despite favorable positioning suggests fading form
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: negative: launching first from strong position then fading badly reveals diminished closing punch
rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: neutral: mentioned in prime position but absent from finale, possibly marking rather than sprinting
